title: Episode 505: The Great American Bash 2008 REMIX
podcast_name: Something to Wrestle with Bruce Prichard
summary: Today's episode comes to you from the Blue Chew studio. Get 10% off your first month of BlueChew Gold with code Wrestle at http://BlueChew.com   On this episode of Something To Wrestle, Bruce Prichard and Conrad Thompson pull back the curtain on the chaos, controversy, and creative decisions surrounding The Great American Bash 2008. Bruce dives deep into the backstage environment at WWE during this time and explains the reasoning behind one of the most talked-about moves in wrestling broadcasting the decision to remove Jim Ross from Monday Night RAW. What really led to that call, and how did JR handle it behind the scenes?  The guys also break down the creation of McMahon's Million Dollar Mania, the fan interactive concept that had Vince McMahon giving away real cash on live television. How did the idea come together? What was happening internally at WWE that made this the direction they went? And what was the reaction from the locker room and production team when the boss started dialing random fans during RAW?  Plus, Bruce shares his candid thoughts on how much influence talent should have in developing their own storylines, the balance between creative direction and performer input, and what worked and didn't during this unique period in WWE history.  If you love hearing the real stories behind the decisions that shaped WWE television, this episode is absolutely loaded with insight, humor, and behind the scenes perspective. There's a lot of meat on this bone, and Bruce isn't holding back on this jam packed edition of Something To Wrestle. title: The Unlucky Origin & Superstitions Behind Friday The 13th
summary: It’s Friday the 13th… so what better day to dig into the real story behind the most infamous date on the calendar? Chris Jericho welcomes geologist, forensic investigator, and history researcher Scott Wolter to break down the origins of Friday the 13th, the truth about the number 13, and why it became one of the most feared superstitions in the world. Scott explains how the legend connects to the downfall of the Knights Templar in 1307, why the number 13 may actually be a powerful and positive number tied to the Fibonacci sequence and the sacred feminine, and how centuries of myth-making turned it into a symbol of bad luck. He also discusses his controversial research into the Kensington Runestone, hidden history connected to the Templars, the discovery of the Talpiot tomb in Jerusalem, and why Friday the 13th still sparks fear, fascination, and superstition more than 700 years later. title: Stay Gold - C. Thomas Howell's 50 Years In Hollywood
summary: Actor C. Thomas Howell joins with incredible behind-the-scenes stories from The Outsiders! Hear how he landed the role of Ponyboy Curtis, the unique workshopping they did that served as auditions, what it was like working with Francis Ford Coppola, Patrick Swayze, Tom Cruise, Matt Dillon, and Ralph Macchio, and how some of the most iconic moments in the film were improvised (like the famous “There’s a monster” moment outside the church)!  Tommy also talks about appearing in Steven Spielberg’s E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial at just 13-years-old, and why filming that movie was like stepping onto a top-secret movie set run by the C.I.A.! He reveals the unbelievable story about being cast as Marty McFly in Back to the Future for two weeks before the role ultimately went to Michael J. Fox. And he discusses his brand-new action films One Mile: Chapter One and One Mile: Chapter Two, shooting two movies simultaneously, playing the villain, and working alongside Ryan Phillippe. Plus, stories about Celebrity Cadabra, filming with Elizabeth Taylor, lessons learned from Rutger Hauer, the underrated film That Night, and the real secret to longevity in Hollywood! ONE MILE: CHAPTER ONE and ONE MILE: CHAPTER TWO are available on Digital now! title: On The Road with Fozzy on the Eyes On You UK Tour
summary: Chris Jericho is flying solo and breaking down Fozzy’s just-wrapped Eyes On You UK Tour! He shares stories from packed shows in Manchester, Nottingham, Newcastle, London, and Wolverhampton, and how several cities delivered the biggest Fozzy crowds they’ve ever had. Chris talks about the Manchester show that nearly derailed due to a massive technical glitch before the band hit the stage, climbing an amp stack and jumping off it during the encore in Newcastle, and watching the Super Bowl in an English pub. He also shares backstage stories about hanging with Tailgunner and Marisa And The Moths, meeting up  with friends like Adrian Smith, Phil Campbell, and KK Downing, the fan VIP jam sessions, the wild after-parties, the movie excursion, and the moments that made this UK run one of the most memorable tours of Fozzy’s career. title: Live From The Lizzie Borden House - America's Most Notorious Axe Murderer
summary: Rob, Dave and Jesse from Hometown Ghost Stories join Chris Jericho LIVE inside the real Lizzie Borden House in Fall River, Massachusetts to break down the infamous 1892 Lizzie Borden axe murders, one of the most brutal and debated true crime cases in American history. Andrew Borden and Abby Borden were hacked to death with a hatchet inside this house. Lizzie Borden was arrested, put on trial, and acquitted after just 90 minutes of jury deliberation!  They dissect the full timeline of the Lizzie Borden case including the mysterious 90-minute gap between killings, Lizzie’s conflicting alibis, the alleged prussic acid poisoning attempt, the burned dress controversy, the hatchet found in the basement, and the shocking autopsy details performed inside the home. They break down the prosecution’s four key pieces of evidence, the questionable police work, the media frenzy that turned this into one of America’s first national true crime sensations, and why the jury came back with a not guilty verdict so quickly. They also examine alternate suspects including Uncle John Morse, inheritance motives, the staged robbery theory, and whether Lizzie’s sister  Emma or maid Bridget Sullivan knew more than they admitted. Plus, because this was filmed inside one of the most haunted houses in America, they explore the paranormal side of the Lizzie Borden House including reported full-body apparitions of Abby Borden, shadow sightings of Andrew Borden, EVPs believed to be Lizzie herself, and the infamous basement corner linked to scratches and dark activity. Is this one of the greatest unsolved mysteries in true crime history? Or did history get it wrong? Watch and decide. title: The Old School Heavy Metal Blitz of Tailgunner
summary: Tailgunner is leading the new wave of British heavy metal, and the buzz is real… they were selling out shows before they’d even played live! Singer Tom Hewson, bassist Zack McEwen, and guitarist Craig Cairns talk about forming the band online during lockdown, betting on themselves by recording and touring independently, and quickly landing runs with W.A.S.P., HammerFall, U.D.O., and Accept. They break down the moment Judas Priest legend KK Downing discovered them, invited them to open his show, and went on to produce their latest album “Midnight Blitz.” They share stories from the road, including wild European crowds singing guitar melodies back to them, the nickname “The Puppies” given to them by Saxon, and their mission to bring classic metal back for a new generation! Plus they talk stage style, attitude, growing global fanbase, and their plans to take over the States next. title: Oh My Gob! An In Depth Deliberation with John Goblikon
summary: John Goblikon of Nekrogoblikon joins to break down his life as a 30,000-year-old goblin navigating humans, music, comedy, and podcasting! John explains what goblins REALLY are (and why trolls are the enemy), growing up in Reseda with a human family, getting bullied in high school, and how a corporate sales job unexpectedly changed his life. He shares the insane story behind Nekrogoblikon’s viral “No One Survives” video (thanks to  Diplo), how it blew up the internet, and how he went from band mascot to frontman (thanks to Post Malone). Plus, John talks about launching his own podcast “Right Now with John Goblikon,” building a stand-up comedy career (including bombing… or were audiences just following goblin rules?), performing at major comedy festivals, and his surprising connection to wrestling, including a wild TNA appearance and why he wants to settle his beef with trolls in the squared circle! He also gets into dating as a goblin, fame vs. authenticity, touring the world with metal fans, and why 2026 might include his own sitcom. All that… and Chris tries to figure out where goblins actually come from. Watch “Right Now with John Goblikon”: https://johngoblikon.com/  Talk Is Jericho is available on YouTube! https://www.youtube.com/ChrisJerichoFozzy  Don’t forget to Like, Subscribe, and hit the🔔to stay updated on new episodes! title: Episode 502: No Way Out 2006
podcast_name: Something to Wrestle with Bruce Prichard
summary: On this episode of Something to Wrestle, Bruce Prichard and Conrad Thompson dive headfirst into No Way Out 2006, a pivotal stop on the road to WrestleMania 22.  From the brutal main event between Kurt Angle and The Undertaker to the emotional and career defining performance of Rey Mysterio, the guys peel back the curtain on one of WWE's most underrated PPVs. Bruce shares behind the scenes insight on creative decisions, locker room dynamics, and what really went into shaping the SmackDown brand at the time.  They also break down Randy Orton's controversial storyline, the fallout from Eddie Guerrero's passing, and how No Way Out 2006 set the tone for multiple WrestleMania moments to come. Honest, unfiltered, and packed with insider details this is Something to Wrestle at its absolute best. title: Dolph Lundgren on Drago, Stallone & 40 Years of Rocky IV
summary: Dolph Lundgren joins to talk about the making of Rocky IV and the creation of one of the most iconic villains in movie history – Ivan Drago! Dolph shares the story of getting dismissed at his first audition for being “too tall,” how he eventually landed the role, and how Sylvester Stallone completely reimagined Drago after meeting him. He breaks down his screen test, the acting choices that made Drago so cold and calculated, and how lines like “I will break you” became part of the pop culture lexicon. Dolph talks training for the film, taking real hits in the ring, and the subtle layers he brought to Drago that made the character more than just a machine. He also discusses his early days as a karate champion and chemical engineer, how he walked away from a Fulbright scholarship at MIT to pursue acting, and the wild journey from a small role in a James Bond film to overnight fame after Rocky IV. Plus, Dolph has stories about Universal Soldier, working with Jean-Claude Van Damme, his run in direct-to-video action films, directing his own movies, and his return to the big screen with The Expendables and Creed II. And… he introduces his new Hard Cut Vodka, and explains what he’s learning about being CEO of his own business! title: The Death Of Superman Lives!
summary: Brad Gilmore returns to break down the wild, unbelievable story behind the Superman movie that never happened (which is also chronicled in the documentary, The Death Of Superman Lives). Tim Burton directing. Nicolas Cage as Superman. Kevin Smith writing the script. And somehow… it all fell apart. Brad and Chris dig into how this massive production got all the way to pre-production (costumes built, sets designed, millions spent) before being shut down just weeks before filming. They get into Kevin Smith’s  original script, producer Jon Peters’ infamous “no tights, no flying, giant spider” rules, and how multiple rewrites, competing visions, and studio panic derailed everything. They also break down Nicolas Cage’s unique take on Superman, Tim Burton’s darker vision, the insane casting ideas (Sandra Bullock, Chris Rock, Christopher Walken), and the leaked costume photos that killed the movie before it even started. Plus, what Batman & Robin had to do with the plug getting pulled, how close this movie really was to shooting, and why this might be the most fascinating “what if” in Hollywood history. title: Episode 413: Minority Report
summary: On this LIVE edition of 83 Weeks, Eric Bischoff and host Conrad Thompson pull back the curtain on the business of professional wrestling past, present, and controversial.  Eric breaks down the science behind booking major Saturday night specials like Clash of the Champions, explaining why timing, competition, and audience behavior matter more than fans realize. He also dives into the real challenges of tape delayed programming, using AEW Grand Slam as a case study in how momentum can shift when spoilers hit.  Bischoff tackles the ongoing fan criticism surrounding creative, making the case that the process constantly evolves and why laying blame at the feet of Triple H oversimplifies a much bigger machine. He doesn't hold back when addressing Dave Meltzer and the recent Brody King reporting controversy, offering his unfiltered take on how narratives get shaped and sometimes distorted.  Plus, Eric weighs in on the business realities behind Warner Bros. Discovery's minority stake in All Elite Wrestling, and the truth behind sluggish ticket sales for WrestleMania what's perception, what's strategy, and what's simply the modern marketplace at work. title: “Garden Shears Are Great” – Favorite Friday The 13 Kills with Roth & Leone
summary: Eli Roth and Damien Leone return on Friday The 13th for a killer episode breaking down their all-time favorite kills from the Friday the 13th film franchise, including the shocking arrow-through-the-neck debut of Kevin Bacon in the OG Friday the 13th to the savage sleeping bag smash in Friday the 13th Part VII: The New Blood. They revisit the wheelchair machete massacre in Friday the 13th Part 2, the head-crushing brutality and corkscrew carnage of Friday the 13th: The Final Chapter, the garden shears to the eyes in Friday the 13th: A New Beginning, the rooftop head punch in Friday the 13th Part VIII: Jason Takes Manhattan, and the liquid nitrogen face smash in Jason X. They also debate practical effects vs. CGI, Tom Savini’s make-up and special efx legacy, the kills that were cut by the MPAA, and the moments that made them want to become filmmakers in the first place. Plus, Damien reveals the kill he thinks might be the most graphic in the entire series, Eli explains why one decapitation changed slashers forever, and Chris shares the death that still makes him cringe. title: Thekla - The Toxic Spider Spins Into AEW
summary: Austrian-born AEW star Thekla breaks down her wild, unconventional path to All Elite Wrestling – from underground training in Vienna cellars with no ring or ropes, to surviving five years in Japan’s brutal wrestling system. Thekla talks about cutting her teeth in Ice Ribbon, leveling up in Stardom, and why living at the dojo during the pandemic accelerated her growth faster than anything else in her career. She explains how touring Europe in a punk band prepared her for life on the road, why punk rock and pro wrestling are  more alike than people realize, and how chaos, rebellion, and DIY culture shaped her in-ring identity. Thekla also details how she first connected with AEW, her initial meeting with Tony Khan, and what it was like walking into the AEW locker room as the new, Japan-trained wildcard. She opens up about creating her signature spider walk, blending flexibility with character work, and using her outsider status as an advantage. Plus, Thekla talks about her AEW faction Triangle of Madness, working alongside Julia Hart and Skye Blue, her goals for championship gold, and why 2026 is officially “the year of the spider.”  Follow Thekla:  📸 Instagram: @toxic_thekla 🐦 X/Twitter: @toxic_thekla Talk Is Jericho is available on YouTube! https://www.youtube.com/ChrisJerichoFozzy  👍 Don’t forget to Like, Subscribe, and hit the 🔔 to stay updated on new episodes!   More from Talk Is Jericho: 📸 Instagram: @chrisjerichofozzy | @talkisjericho 🐦 Twitter/X: @IAmJericho | @TalkIsJericho 🌐 Website: https://www.webisjericho.com/    To learn more about listener data and our privacy practices visit: https://www.audacyinc.com/privacy-policy     Learn more about your ad choices. title: How Big Daddy & Giant Haystacks Made… and Slayed British Wrestling
summary: Author Steven Bell returns to break down the real story behind his new book Big Daddy vs Haystacks and the rivalry that took over British wrestling (and television). He explains how Big Daddy (aka Shirley Crabtee, Jr) and Giant Haystacks (aka Martin Ruane) became massive pop-culture icons, why their feud pulled in millions of viewers every week, and how years of tension finally exploded in their legendary Wembley Arena showdown – one of the most watched wrestling matches in UK history! Steven reveals how the rivalry was stretched out on TV for years, what really happened in the ring at Wembley, and why that match became both the high point and the turning point of the British wrestling boom. He also gets into their connections to WWE – why Big Daddy never crossed over, how Giant Haystacks later surfaced in the States as “Loch Ness,” and what their brief American runs exposed about the clash between British wrestling and the American style. Plus, Steven shares behind-the-scenes stories from Stampede Wrestling, including what Bret Hart really thought of Haystacks during his time in Canada, and how those Stampede matches helped shape the legend, and the mythology of Giant Haystacks outside the UK. Pre-order Big Daddy Versus Giant Haystacks: A Super Heavyweight Story: https://a.co/d/05JwG1qg  An exclusive, limited stock of signed copies is now available! title: Caroline Cory Explains Superhuman Science & The Buga Sphere
summary: Award-winning filmmaker, futurist, and consciousness researcher Caroline Cory breaks down the mysterious Buga Sphere (the strange metallic object found in Mexico made from 12,000-year-old material). She explains why scientists still can’t debunk it, where it is now, and what it could mean for non-human intelligence. Caroline talks about her documentary A Tear in the Sky and the real-time anomaly her team captured over the California coast, how Tic Tac UAPs forced the Pentagon to admit the existence of UAPs, and why she believes the disclosure is happening now. She explains telekinesis, remote viewing, and why she believes human  are capable of abilities we’ve been trained to use (like moving objects with our minds and changing the pH balance in water). Caroline details the CIA’s Stargate Program, and how remote viewing was used for intelligence gathering. Plus, she predicts what’s coming next for humanity, why governments may soon acknowledge non-human intelligence, and how consciousness could be the key to understanding both UFO technology and human potential. And then there’s her own personal UFO story. title: Crowbar – From Physical Extremist to Physical Therapist
summary: Former WCW wrestler Crowbar (aka Chris Ford) tells the unbelievable story of how he went from gothic hardcore wrestler to real-life physical therapist who helps injured wrestlers get back in the ring. Chris talks about getting into wrestling while still in high school, juggling college and WCW bookings, and the advice Diamond Dallas Page gave him that changed the course of his career. He explains how the Crowbar gimmick was created, what it was like teaming with David Flair and Daphne, and why working with Terry Funk was one of the biggest moments of his life. Crowbar also shares stories from WCW’s final chaotic years, wrestling Chris Benoit, Vampiro, and Sabu, and why his run ended just before the company folded. Plus, he reveals how his second career as a licensed physical therapist led him to rehabbing wrestlers like Joey Janela and AEW’s Brian Cage, why Cage flew across the country to work with him, and how knowing the human body changed the way he wrestles today. title: The History of the Royal Rumble (1988–2025)
summary: John Pollock goes through the History of the Royal Rumble from pre-1988 through 2025, from a failed house show concept to a major cable program, and being added as its annual January show. Pollock chats about the origins of the match, the pivotal role it played in 1988 against Jim Crockett Promotions, becoming an annual pay-per-view in 1989, its growth as a draw on the WWE’s calendar, the highs & lows along the way, and the chronology through last year’s show in Indianapolis. title: After The Fame – Growing Up Nelson
summary: Matthew and Gunnar Nelson tell the true story behind their famous last name – growing up as the sons of Ricky Nelson and the grandsons of Ozzie & Harriet, and why their childhood was nothing like the sitcom America thought it was. They open up about their turbulent family life, losing their father in a tragic plane crash, and why the long-standing rumors about that crash are completely wrong. They also break down their own meteoric rise in the MTV era with “(Can’t Live Without Your) Love and Affection,” what it was like becoming teen heartthrobs overnight, and why being judged by their image nearly derailed their music careers. Matthew and Gunnar talk about being written off as “trust fund kids,” how they really made their money (and lost it), the brutal realities of the record business, and how their bond as twins kept them going. They also share incredible stories about Prince complimenting their style, Henry Rollins becoming an unlikely ally, and how a James Gunn connection landed their music on the DC’s Peacemaker on HBOMax. Plus, they dive deep into their new book, What Happened To Your Hair, a brutally honest, funny, and emotional look at fame, family, tragedy, and resilience, and why now was finally the time to tell their story in their own words. title: [BONUS] Tanahashi vs. Okada Retrospective | REWIND-A-WAI #198
summary: Enjoy the first 30 minutes of this POSTwrestlingCafe.com Exclusive Podcast — Following their final encounter at Wrestle Kingdom 20, John Pollock and Wai Ting revisit the era-defining rivalry between Hiroshi Tanahashi and Kazuchika Okada through a selection of key matches from their 2012-2016 saga. They discuss Okada’s early years, from his mishandled TNA excursion as Okato to his 2012 NJPW homecoming that produced the “Rainmaker Shock”, when booker Gedo made the controversial call to have the then-unproven Okada upset long-reigning champion Tanahashi for the IWGP Heavyweight Title. John & Wai contextualize their subsequent series of critically acclaimed matches, tracing Okada’s epic journey as he attempts, fails, and ultimately succeeds in being recognized by the audience and Tanahashi as the true “Ace” of the promotion. Matches covered (NJPW World account required):The New Beginning (2/12/12)Wrestle Kingdom 7 (1/4/13)Invasion Attack (4/7/13)Wrestle Kingdom 9 (1/4/15)Wrestle Kingdom 10 (1/4/16)This episode was brought to you by Espresso Executive Producer, Robbie Ellinor Next time on Rewind-A-Wai: Shinya Hashimoto Retrospective Check out the Rewind-A-Wai Archives for all patrons: https://www.postwrestling.com/category/podcasts/postwrestlingcafe/raw/ Bluesky: https://bsky.app/profile/postwrestling.com X: http://www.twitter.com/POSTwrestling Instagram: http://www.instagram.com/POSTwrestling Facebook: http://www.facebook.com/POSTwrestling YouTube: http://www.youtube.com/POSTwrestling Subscribe: https://postwrestling.com/subscribe Patreon: http://postwrestlingcafe.com Forum: https://forum.postwrestling.com Discord: https://postwrestling.com/discord See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

title: Episode 498: Royal Rumble 2001

summary: On this episode of Something To Wrestle, Bruce and Conrad rewind the clock 25 years to revisit Royal Rumble 2001 a pivotal moment in wrestling history. Bruce shares never-before-told stories surrounding the purchase of WCW, gives an inside look at what it was really like working as a writer in WWE during that era, and breaks down why the in-ring chemistry just didn't click between Triple H and his Olympic gold medal–winning opponent. title: The Low Down on D'Lo Brown
summary: Former WWE Intercontinental Champion D'Lo Brown takes it back to the Smoky Mountain Wrestling days – flophouse living with Jericho, New Jack, Balls Mahoney, and a house full of future stars – before breaking down his rapid rise to WWE and landing in the Nation of Domination. He talks working closely with Ron Simmons, road-tripping with The Rock before superstardom, earning trust during the Attitude Era, and becoming the first-ever Euro-Continental Champion. title: You Thought You Knew Kevin Federline... But You Don't
summary: Kevin Federline joins to talk about his new memoir You Thought You Knew, and explains why it took years (and his kids’ approval) before he felt ready to write the book, and set the record straight. Kevin talks about working as a dancer with Michael Jackson, including unforgettable behind-the-scenes stories from the You Rock My World video, rehearsing to a click track, MJ’s perfectionism, and seeing the King of Pop’s playful side off camera (with Marlon Brando). He also shares stories from touring with Pink and LFO, life on the road as a young dancer, and the pressure of performing night after night in front of massive crowds. He discusses meeting Britney Spears, how quickly their relationship exploded into global chaos, and what it was really like being married to one of the most famous people in the world at the height of paparazzi culture. Kevin speaks candidly about the media narrative surrounding him, the impact on his mental health, the breakdown of their marriage, and how everything ultimately changed once kids were involved. He also relives his surprising and successful run in WWE, including getting nuclear heat from the crowd, working with John Cena, earning Vince McMahon’s trust, and discovering just how brutal the wrestling business really  is both physically and mentally. title: The Unsolved Pursuit of D.B. Cooper
summary: John and Jamie from the True Crimecast return for a true crime first…the first non-murder case they’ve ever covered on the show! It’s one of the most famous unsolved mysteries of all time: D.B. Cooper! They break it down from the beginning – the night before Thanksgiving 1971, when a well-dressed man using the name Dan Cooper boarded a Northwest Orient flight, ordered a bourbon, passed a flight attendant a note claiming he had a bomb, demanded $200,000 and four parachutes, and then jumped out of a Boeing 727 into the dark, stormy Pacific Northwest… never to be seen again. They detail exactly what happened on the plane, why Cooper seemed to have inside knowledge of the aircraft, parachutes, altitude, and flight speed, and how he calmly pulled off the only unsolved hijacking in U.S. history. They also dig into the FBI investigation, the missing and mysteriously destroyed evidence, the ransom money found years later along the Columbia River, and why authorities ultimately closed the case. Plus, John and Jamie run through the top suspects, including military paratroopers, pilots, con men, and all the alleged deathbed confessions. They dissect why some believe Cooper never survived, why others are convinced he got away clean, and ultimately where they land on the mystery. title: Q Talks Impractical Jokers, Hypothermia & Humiliations
summary: Brian “Q” Quinn from Impractical Jokers makes his first-ever TIJ video appearance! He revisits the infamous ice-bath punishment that left him with real hypothermia, explains how close things came to going seriously wrong, and admits which punishments still mess with him years later. He explains why mental punishments are often way worse than physical ones, and why Murr is always up for ANYTHING! Q breaks down the evolution of Impractical Jokers, why they never expected it to last 15 years, and how the show changed after Joe Gatto stepped away. Q also talks honestly about the bond with Sal Vulcano and James Murray, why Joe was never officially “replaced,” and how celebrities became the next chapter. And Q dives into his love of horror, working with his hero Bruce Campbell, and recreating Evil Dead 2 for a Jokers punishment. Plus, he talks acting outside of comedy, getting “killed” on screen, and developing a new horror movie that not only takes him completely out of his comfort zone but also may or may not involve Chris Jericho! title: 60 Years Of Star Trek & Chekov with Walter Koenig
summary: Walter Koenig takes us inside the creation of Chekov on Star Trek – from nearly not existing at all to becoming central to one of the franchise’s most iconic moments in The Wrath of Khan. He explains how he landed the role after being asked to play a life-or-death scene funny, and why that unexpected choice helped define the character. Walter goes deep on The Wrath of Khan, including Chekov’s pivotal role, working with Ricardo Montalbán, and why the film still stands as one of the strongest Star Trek stories ever told. He also reveals how close Star Trek came to cancellation, Gene Roddenberry’s constant battles with the network, and the realities of being typecast including long stretches of unemployment after the series ended. Walter talks about how writing helped save his career, how he ended up writing for Star Trek himself, and what it was really like inside the original cast hierarchy. Plus, Comic Con fandom 60 years later, The Trouble With Tribbles, The Voyage Home, and why – at 88 years old – he’s taking on the most challenging role of his life in the powerful new short film Mr. Fleischman. title: Captain and Tennille Christmas Watchalong aka The Bosley Clause
summary: PJ Farley and the Pod of Thunder crew (Andy, Nick, and the mysterious Chris L) return for the most unhinged holiday watchalong yet as they hit PLAY on the Captain & Tennille Christmas Special that originally aired on ABC in December of 1976! What follows is a full-on descent into network TV madness featuring Don Knotts chain-smoking his way through an airplane sketch, Tom Bosley (aka Howard Cunningham from Happy Days) siting through his contributions to the show, the Pointer Sisters bringing the little energy that existed, and The Captain (aka Daryl Dragon) looking like the most miserable man ever trapped behind a camera (or a piano for that matter). The guys break down the baffling guest list, the nonstop ballads, the total lack of Christmas spirit, and the awkward 70s variety-show chaos – all in real time. It’s uncomfortable, confusing, unintentionally hilarious, and possibly the least joyful holiday special ever made! title: Tia Carrere Sings Ronnie James Dio!
summary: Actress and musician Tia Carrere shares stories about the role that changed her life - Cassandra in Wayne’s World! She explains how Mike Myers personally told her she was his pick, how she learned to play bass and sing like Pat Benatar for the movie, and why the soundtrack became such a massive part of the film’s success. Tia has behind-the-scenes stories from True Lies including auditioning for James Cameron, working with Arnold Schwarzenegger, Jamie Lee Curtis, and Bill Paxton, and the bold post-production decision she made that completely changed her character, Juno Skinner. She also talks about winning two GRAMMY Awards for Hawaiian music, her love of rock and metal (hear her impromptu Black Sabbath and Ronnie James Dio singing), and why music has always been at the center of her career. Tia discusses Lilo & Stitch, the emotional reason the story still resonates more than 20 years later, and what it means to help bring Hawaiian culture to audiences around the world. Plus, she talks about being discovered in Hawaii, her early days on General Hospital, almost becoming a regular on The A-Team, improvising on Curb Your Enthusiasm, and traveling the world on Relic Hunter. title: How SEAL Team Six Captured Osama Bin Laden
summary: Former Navy SEAL Robert O’Neill tells the full, uncensored story of the mission that ended with the capture and killing of Osama bin Laden. Robert breaks down what really happened inside SEAL Team Six, the years-long hunt for the world’s most wanted terrorist , and the moment the call finally came that changed everything. He takes us inside the planning, the secrecy, and the mindset of accepting a mission he believed he might not come back from – including the 90 minute flight into Pakistan, the helicopter crash inside the compound that nearly gave them away, and the split-second decisions made inside bin Laden’s house. Robert shares the emotional weight of the mission, what it meant for the families affected by 9/11, and why this was never about glory!    Watch The Operator with Rob O’Neill on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLDIOHplBXr75eHNTtMwqXDkwklXKM5USc   Talk Is Jericho is available on YouTube! https://www.youtube.com/ChrisJerichoFozzy 👍 Don’t forget to Like, Subscribe, and hit the 🔔 to stay updated on new episodes! title: [BONUS] Video Killed The Radio Star (2025) Review | DOUBLE SHOT
summary: Enjoy this free preview from the POST Wrestling Café — John Pollock and David Bixenspan review Video Killed The Radio Star, covering the sex tape scandal involving Bubba the Love Sponge and the late Hulk Hogan.  They discuss the sex tape scandal involving Bubba the Love Sponge and Hulk Hogan, the legal fallout, Bubba’s rivals in the Tampa radio market, and how the documentary presents Bubba’s side of the story.  The landscape of the Tampa radio scene and Bubba’s roleThe rise of Bubba the Love Sponge and his popularity peakLeaving terrestrial radio for Howard 101Hogan’s usage of Bubba’s platform and how often he was calling inBubba’s explanation for so many cameras around his houseThe circumstances of the sex tape getting out The biggest omission throughout the doc Who are painted as the culprits?The FBI sting operation in December 2012Gawker’s role and its demise after the trial Tying Bubba to Donald Trump and a victim of cancel cultureBubba’s theory of why those responsible were not prosecuted What happened in the last 90 minutes? Listen to the rest of this podcast: https://www.patreon.com/posts/145633304 Synopsis: “When a sex tape featuring Hulk Hogan and the wife of his best friend —radio shock jock Bubba the Love Sponge® Clem—was leaked online, it ignited a media firestorm. You’ve read the headlines. Bubba recorded it. Gawker published it. This shocking documentary unpacks the lies, the betrayals, and the lawsuit that rewrote the rules of privacy. But the resulting fallout had much greater consequences than just Hogan crushing Gawker. You think you know the real story. You don’t know anything.” The DOUBLE SHOT is where John & Wai review things that don’t really fit on their other shows. title: Wu Tang For Life with Method Man
summary: Wu-Tang legend Method Man returns to Talk Is Jericho to talk about his massive comic book collection (including key issues worth serious money), how he became a full-blown horror movie fan (Evil Dead II, Dead Alive, and deep-cut horror trivia included), and why horror-con fans might be the most passionate fans on Earth. Method breaks down the origins of Wu-Tang Clan, how the group scraped together studio money in the early days, the genius strategy behind their groundbreaking record deal, and what it was really like being part of Wu-Tang’s recent “final” tour. Method also shares powerful memories of ODB, and explains why ODB was the soul of Wu-Tang, and why his energy can never be replaced. Plus, Method gets real about his acting career, how music opened the door to Hollywood, what he learned working alongside Samuel L. Jackson, why he humbled himself by sleeping on his manager’s couch, and why acting has now become his main creative focus. And if you listen closely, you might catch Method leading a little spontaneous doo-wop sing-a-long! title: Maria Cuccio – Alien Abduction, Missing Time and the Boy Called Elijah
summary: Maria Cuccio was a married Long Island piano teacher and mother of three when she woke up in the middle of the night in 1992 to a blinding white light! She felt her body lifted by a beam, and found herself aboard a spacecraft surrounded by tall robed beings. She was shown a group of children, including an 8-year-old boy she was told was her son, Elijah! Maria recounts the unbelievable experience, and explains the profound impact it had on her life. She details the Long Island and Montauk UFO activity of the 1980s and 90s, the reactions of her husband, children, doctors, and psychiatrists, and the decades she spent journaling her experiences in silence before finally speaking out. Maria also discusses suppressed childhood memories, missing time, unexplained disappearances, government disclosure, UAPs, and why she believes thousands of women around the world share variations of her same story. title: Classic Album Clash – KISS “Lick It Up” vs “Asylum”
summary: Kuarantine’s PJ Farley and Charlie Parra along with drummer Joey Cassata return for the KISS Classic Album Clash, and this time it’s Lick It Up vs. Asylum! They break down two of KISS’ most iconic non-makeup records track by track… and no song, solo, drum fill, or album cover is safe! They’re diving into the post-apocalyptic Lick It Up reveal, the neon madness of Asylum, Gene’s “checked-out” 80s narrative (and why it may be dead wrong), and the songs that nearly saved (or sank) the band. Hear how Rick Derringer ended up shredding on “Exciter,” why Eric Carr secretly wrote the riff to “All Hell’s Breakin’ Loose,” how “Who Wants To Be Lonely” became a surprising fan favorite, and why Joey once sprinted through a mall just to see KISS without makeup for the first time. And of course, they each reveal their final vote for which album comes out on top when all is said and done! title: Spooks & Scares From The Paranormal Highway with Dustin Pari
summary: Dustin Pari of Ghost Hunters fame does a paranormal deep dive that’s even wilder than the conversation Chris and Dustin lost to “technical difficulties” at Scarefest! From the shadow figure that set him on his life path at age 9 to the lady in white at the St. Augustine Lighthouse, the air base hospital in the Philippines, and the moment trickster spirit physically knocked him off his feet in Ireland, Dustin breaks down 33 years of investigating the unexplained. He talks EVPs, ghost boxes, elemental spirits, portals, alien communication, how music opens the door between worlds, and why he believes spirits see us as light. Dustin also shares stories about the infamous doll Annabelle, Robert the Doll, the Bridgewater Triangle, the Anderson Hotel, and the night a ghost box eerily replied, “watching from above.” Plus, Dustin reveals why he believes the afterlife is closer than we think, and why he’s not scared of what comes next. title: KISS Landlocked LIVE in Las Vegas with Chris Jericho
summary: You wanted the best… you got the best! LIVE from KISS Kruise Landlocked in Vegas! Chris Jericho hosts an epic fan Q&A with Paul Stanley, Gene Simmons, Tommy Thayer & Eric Singer, packed with unheard stories, backstage confessions, onstage mishaps, deep-cut lore, and massive teases about the future of KISS. Paul and Gene open up about getting back onstage after two years, the chemistry that still fires the band, and why they insist this moment isn’t “the sunset” but merely a new beginning for KISS! They also break down everything from the origin of the makeup to the Tom Snyder chaos to the band’s first-ever use of a drum riser (which was literally powered by a forklift!) The guys talk dangerous fire-spitting mishaps, flying-rig disasters, the truth behind the avatars, the legacy of Ace Frehley & Peter Criss, why tough love matters, and how KISS went from a rat-infested New York loft to a half-century empire of fans across generations. Plus, Paul and Gene reveal whether new music might be in KISS’ future, and spill a few details on future KISS projects! title: Matt Pinfield’s Rock & Roll Resurrection!
summary: Matt Pinfield finally joins TIJ to detail two unbelievable life-or-death moments that changed everything - the Hollywood accident that sent him through a windshield, and the massive stroke and MRSA pneumonia doctors said he’d never survive. He talks waking up after two months unconscious, the brutal rehab, the rock music that fueled his comeback, and the gold-plated cane Ozzy sent him from England during recovery. Matt also chronicles his early days in music from Jersey rock-radio diehard to MTV’s 120 Minutes host and champion of Nirvana, Green Day, Oasis, Korn, Limp Bizkit, Deftones and more. He shares how one bold phone call landed him the MTV gig, why Depeche Mode nearly derailed his first audition, and what it was really like hanging with Metallica, Van Halen, and hosting his own version of Headbangers Ball called MATTROCK!  Plus, hear the incredible story about the day he ended up singing KISS classics with Gene Simmons, Peter Criss, and the late, great Ace Frehley.   Talk Is Jericho is available on YouTube! https://www.youtube.com/ChrisJerichoFozzy 👍 Don’t forget to Like, Subscribe, and hit the 🔔 to stay updated on new episodes! title: Viva Van's Hellbent Journey to Viet Nam... and AEW
summary: AEW’s Hellbent Vixen Viva Van dives into her unique journey from Little Saigon to All Elite Wrestling, and the unbelievable story of how she helped Vietnam Pro Wrestling get their very first wrestling ring. Viva breaks down the grassroots movement that brought pro wrestling to Vietnam, the GoFundMe that changed everything, and what it meant to wrestle in Ho Chi Minh City for the very first time. She talks about her WWE private tryout, how the rejection lit a fire under her, her grind across the indies, training at the NJPW LA Dojo, and the moment she found out (at the same time as the fans) that she was about to wrestle on AEW Dynamite. Viva also shares amazing stories about wrestling Timeless Toni Storm on AEW Rampage, her metal-inspired ring entrance, her time singing in a death-metal band, discovering wrestling through video games, and growing up speaking only Vietnamese. Plus, she reveals her dream matches in AEW and Japan. title: Survivor Series 89 & 90. The Hulkamaniacs and The Perfect Team & Ax’s Perfect Hair?
summary: Get ready for a deep dive into one of the wildest stretches of Demolition history. In this episode of DemoPOD: The Demolition Podcast, Ax & Smash break down their back-to-back WWF Survivor Series appearances in 1989 and 1990 — two years that saw Demolition standing both alongside and across the ring from WWE legends like Hulk Hogan, The Ultimate Warrior, Mr. Perfect, and more.We dig into the stories behind The Perfect Team, the chemistry (and chaos) of those massive Survivor Series elimination matches, locker room memories from the road, and what it was really like being at the center of the tag team scene at the height of the WWF boom.And yes… we absolutely talk about Bill Eadie’s Perfect hair and how that iconic look came together.If you love late ’80s / early ’90s WWF, Survivor Series nostalgia, or tag team psychology, this is a can’t-miss DemoPOD episode. Hit play and relive the face paint, the power, and the pure Demolition mayhem.#DemoPOD #Demolition #AxAndSmash #WWF #WWE #SurvivorSeries #SurvivorSeries1989 #SurvivorSeries1990 #MrPerfect #HulkHogan #UltimateWarrior #TagTeamWrestling #RetroWrestling #ProWrestlingPodcast #WrestlingNostalgia

title: Survivor Series 87 & 88 Showdowns: Demolition’s 10 Team Wars & The Legendary Double Turn
summary: Demolition ruled Survivor Series. In this episode of DemoPOD, Ax & Smash take you back to the late 80s and break down what really went down behind the paint.We dig into:🔥 Demolition’s Survivor Series debut in 1987 and how it felt walking into that chaotic elimination environment for the first time🔥 Their 1988 return and the evolution of the classic 10-man tag format🔥 Backstage stories, road tales, and the strategy behind those huge Survivor Series tag team matches🔥 The infamous double turn with the Powers of Pain — how it was planned, how the crowd reacted, and what it meant for the entire WWF tag team division🔥 What Ax & Smash remember most from those wild Thanksgiving night arenas and why this era still connects with fans todayIf you’re into late 80s WWF, long-term storytelling, and true tag team psychology, this is one of the most nostalgia-packed DemoPOD episodes yet.Hit play and relive the carnage, the chaos, and the face-painted glory of Demolition at their peak.#Demolition #DemoPOD #WWF #WWE #SurvivorSeries #SurvivorSeries87 #SurvivorSeries88 #PowersOfPain #OldSchoolWrestling #80sWrestling #TagTeamWrestling #ProWrestlingPodcast #WrestlingPodcast #ShootInterview

title: Episode 402: Bret Said What?
summary: On this episode of 83 Weeks, Eric and Conrad unload a jam-packed slate of headlines, controversy, and classic WCW insight. Eric dives into the return of the super cards concept and shares his unfiltered thoughts on Bret Hart's latest barrage of bombastic statements, including what Bret got right—and what he absolutely didn't. The guys also address Dave Meltzer labeling Eric a "grifter," with Bischoff responding the only way he knows how: directly, honestly, and with zero hesitation.  Then, the conversation shifts to a deep dive into the creation and evolution of Raven's Flock. Did Eric see the faction as a unique, gritty piece of WCW storytelling? Or was it an idea he'd have rather left on the cutting room floor? Eric sets the record straight with behind-the-scenes insight you won't hear anywhere else.  All that plus so much more on this can't-miss, no-holds-barred edition of 83 Weeks with Eric Bischoff. title: Blood Runs Cold - The Fiery To Frozen Story of WCW’s Glacier
summary: WCW legend Glacier (aka Ray Lloyd) talks about the creation of his Mortal Kombat–inspired gimmick, the “Blood Runs Cold” vignettes, the expensive laser-and-snow entrance that became his calling card, debuting on WCW Nitro against Big Bubba, the and why Raven saddled him with the nickname “Fat Fingers the Magician.” He gets into his legit martial-arts background, his time fighting in the UWFI system in Japan, and how that all led him to pro wrestling. Ray also recalls working with icons like Lou Thesz and Billy Robinson, his friendship with Canyon and Ernest Miller, learning from Dusty Rhodes, the pressure of coming in with a big push, and how that entire WCW run eventually ended. Plus, he details how a chance reconnection with QT Marshall led to his new life as a coach, mentor, and backstage producer in AEW, and why returning to the business has completely reignited his passion for pro wrestling. title: The History of the Survivor Series (1987 - 2024)
summary: John Pollock covers the history of the Survivor Series event with a year-by-year analysis, beginning in 1987 and going through its many iterations. title: Jackie Earle Haley Is Breaking Back Into Hollywood
summary: Jackie Earle Haley shares stories from his 50 years in Hollywood, making the jump from child star to Oscar-nominated actor, and the wild road that took him from Bad News Bears to delivering pizzas before Sean Penn and Steve Zaillian pulled him back for All The King’s Men. He gets into the audition tape that relaunched his career, the intense process behind Little Children, and the emotional moment he learned he’d been nominated for an Academy Award. He remembers shooting Bad News Bears as Kelly Leak, riding motorcycles at 13, working with Walter Matthau and Tatum O’Neal, and carrying Breaking Training as a teenager. He breaks down playing Rorschach in Watchmen, goes deep on building his version of Freddy Krueger including the brutal daily make-up process, and shares why his new horror film Your Host let him go full “batshit crazy” in the best way possible. title: Episode 489: Violent J and JBL's Insane Top 5 Lists!
podcast_name: Something to Wrestle with Bruce Prichard
summary: On this special Thanksgiving edition of Something To Wrestle, John and Conrad welcome founding member of The Insane Clown Posse, Violent J, to the show! Violent J shares the story of how Juggalo Championship Wrestling was created and how it's grown through the years, plus he gives the guys an inside look at what it's really like working with the infamous Vince Russo — and his honest thoughts on Russo's role in JCW.  It's a fascinating interview that shines a light on JCW's unique place in wrestling and reveals Violent J's passion and dedication to his fans and his promotion. And if that's not enough, John and Conrad take us back through some of JBL's wildest Top 5 lists to wrap up this fun holiday episode! title: Quoth The Raven… Nevermore!
summary: Raven shares why he demanded a “warts and all” story when it came to his new documentary, Nevermore: The Raven Effect. He explains how insecurity shaped his early career, what really happened when he walked away from Vince McMahon and the WWF office, and how five minutes of “divine inspiration” created the Raven character. He and filmmaker Geordie Day talk about the wild reality of ECW in the ’90s, and why the Dreamer feud, Sandman storyline, and Crucifixion angle worked so well. Raven also recounts the truth about his relationship with Paul Heyman, his favorite episodic stories from Portland working for the Grappler, the WrestleMania 17 golf-cart fiasco, the frustrations of WCW and the Flock, the real story behind Shane McMahon staying at his house, and the ending beat of the film that Raven loves the most. title: Eli Roth - The Scoop Behind Ice Cream Man, Dream Eater, and The Horror Section
summary: Eli Roth returns to break down the launch of his film company Horror Section and how he’s building a place for unrated, no-rules genre movies. He shares the story behind shooting his upcoming horror film Ice Cream Man, why he resurrected an idea he first wrote 20 years ago, and the insane on-set challenges that came with directing 100 blood-soaked kids in the Toronto heat. He talks about discovering strange, low-budget gems like Dream Eater, the “third-tape cinema” philosophy driving his new slate, and how Terrifier proved that unrated horror can still break the box office. Eli recalls Quentin Tarantino’s early support on Cabin Fever and Hostel, explains why Quentin insisted Eli play the Bear Jew in Inglourious Basterds, and shares what it was like to watch Tarantino act out every role during early read-throughs. Eli discusses his upcoming appearance on HBO’s Euphoria, working with Sam Levinson, and shooting on 65mm film. Plus, he opens up about navigating studio politics, distributing indie horror, mentoring young filmmakers, pushing himself as both a director and an actor, and what he has planned for Thanksgiving 2! Eli Roth Presents DREAM EATER is available now wherever you stream movies! 📸 Instagram: @horrorsectionstudios | @dreameatermovie Thank you for supporting our sponsors! title: A Hard Rock Holiday with Trans-Siberian Orchestra’s Al Pitrelli
summary: Trans-Siberian Orchestra guitar mastermind Al Pitrelli breaks down three decades of symphonic shred and arena-sized storytelling – from the first time Paul O’Neill pushed up the faders on “Christmas Eve/Sarajevo” to the massive two-band touring machine TSO has become. Al shares the emotional origins of the music, the early days grinding it out with one fog machine and a single box truck, what it’s like stepping onstage in front of 60,000 screaming fans, and revisiting the songs he helped build with Paul and Jon Oliva every year. He also shares stories from his time with Megadeth like learning rhythm guitar at the school of Dave Mustaine, sitting with Marty Friedman practicing night after night to perfect the parts, surviving Dave’s legendary scrutiny, and recording The World Needs a Hero album. Plus, he dives into his years as Alice Cooper’s musical director, where he learned the stagecraft, theatrics, and precision that still guide his work today. title: Heeeeeeeeres Johnny! Behind The Scenes Of Carson’s Tonight Show
summary: Author Mark Malkoff pulls back the curtain on his new book Love Johnny Carson: One Obsessive Fan’s Journey to Find the Genius Behind the Legend — and the wild, never-before-told stories of TV’s ultimate host! Hear how Frank Sinatra saved Johnny from a mob hit, why Joan Rivers’ secret betrayal ended their friendship forever, what Ellen DeGeneres did to allegedly get banned from the show, and the role Carson played in the launch of Saturday Night Live. Mark also reveals Robin Williams’ unfiltered chaos backstage, Rodney Dangerfield’s long road from being banned to beloved, and how Johnny made careers for stars like Burt Reynolds, Drew Carey and Jerry Seinfeld. Plus, stories about the everyday people who appeared on the show, Johnny’s great chemistry with kids, and why animal trainers Joan Embery from the San Diego Zoo and Wild Kingdom’s Jim Fowler and their non-human friends were constant fixtures!
